dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 responding for psychiatric problem at address in [CITY]. Patient is combative, confused.
Hello, I'm Alex with the rescue squad. Can you tell us when you started noticing these changes in him?
I'm Sammy, my partner. We're here to help assess and stabilize him.
He had a mental status change around 11:00 a.m. He became agitated, talked about suicide, and became uncooperative.
Thank you. We'll be doing a quick check of his condition now if that's okay.
Mr. Robert, can you tell us where you are and what day it is?
I'm in my home in [CITY]. I can't feel my arms. I'm so hot.
Can you tell us your full name, Mr. Robert?
Robert Johnson. I can't move my hands. I'm restrained.
Do you mind if I quickly check Mr. Robert's airway for patency?
Airway is patent. No signs of obstruction or distress observed.
Respirations are at 15 per minute, normal depth.
No cyanosis observed, oxygen saturation is at 100% on room air.
Pulse is 102 BPM, regular. Skin is dry, consistent with his condition.
ECG monitoring started to confirm rhythm and cardiac status.
Pulse is 102, regular. No arrhythmia detected.
Respiratory rate is steady at 15 per minute.
Blood pressure is 108/70, low normal for him. Minor variation noted.
Blood glucose is 98 mg/dL. No hypoglycemia detected.
Oxygen saturation is 100% on room air. No need for supplemental oxygen at this time.
ECG shows sinus tachycardia, no ectopy or signs of ischemia detected.
Four-lead ECG monitoring in progress per protocol to continuously assess heart function.
Ma'am, can you tell us about any medical history for Mr. Robert?
Robert has a history of opioid abuse. These episodes seem to happen on Thursdays.
What happened this morning that led to the call today, by the way?
He started acting strange around 11:00 a.m. He became agitated and contacted police for help.
Does he have any pre-existing conditions we should be aware of?
He has a condition called ectodermal dysplasia. He cannot sweat, making heat management difficult.
Does he have asthma or any respiratory conditions listed?
Yes, he has mild asthma, but has not needed his inhaler recently.
Ma'am, do you know if he has any other prescriptions or meds?
Suboxone is the only medication. He doesn't use an inhaler regularly.
Any allergies we should know about?
He is allergic to shellfish.
Mr. Robert, are you in any pain right now?
No pain, but I feel extremely hot. My arms are tethered.
We noticed you are unable to sweat, a trait from birth. Have you noticed this issue today more than usual?
Yes, it's worse. I can't cool down, and I feel more heated than usual.
His mental status is alert but combative. Oriented to self but not to time or place.
Based on the history and presentation, we'll follow protocol 3-4 for behavioral and patient restraint.
Blood pressure now at 114/68, shows slight improvement from the last reading.
Glucose reading was zero, the strips might have expired, will recheck once possible.
Administering 5 mg of midazolam and 10 mg haloperidol IM for sedation and control.
Inserted a nasopharyngeal airway with an oxygen mask set at 10 LPM for supplemental oxygen.
Pulse remains at 102 BPM post-sedation. No rhythm changes detected with ECG monitoring.
Still normal respiratory rate and saturation-no signs of worsening.
Signs of sedation beginning. Mr. Robert is showing decreased resistance and agitation as expected.
We'll transport him to [COMPANY_NAME] for further evaluation.
Ma'am, can you help secure Mr. Robert on the gurney? He is still restrained.
Yes, of course. I can inform the hospital as well.
Please let the hospital know to expect a 36-year-old male psychiatric patient.
He's been secured with strap restraints and is being cooled with an ice sheet.
[CITY], this is Alex with Unit 3. Transporting a 36-year-old male psychiatric patient.
Vital signs: BP 114/68, P 102, RR 15, O2 100%, and no pain reported.
Medicated with midazolam and haloperidol. Currently sedated and not responsive.
GCS was 12 on scene-alert but disoriented.
End-tidal CO2 is elevated at 51 mm Hg, likely from increased metabolic rate.
Patient has been secured using standard restraints. Current medications include Suboxone.
ECG monitoring performed with no evidence of cardiac compromise.
Oxygen and sedation given. Patient is receiving cooling with wet sheet in place.
No traumatic injuries observed during assessment.
[CITY], we're two minutes out with a psychiatric transport.
Temperature remains at 105 degrees F. IV was not possible due to restraints.
No insurance or ID provided. Patient is from [CITY].
